report_name: 'Operation Dragon Weave': China-Linked Espionage Campaign Targets Taiwan and Czech Republic

threat_actors.name: China-linked threat actor
threat_actors.description: A suspected China-linked threat actor responsible for the 'Operation Dragon Weave' cyberespionage campaign, targeting entities in Taiwan and the Czech Republic with customized spearphishing attacks for data exfiltration and remote access.
threat_actors.goals: Data exfiltration and establishing long-term remote access for espionage purposes.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Victims received targeted emails with malicious ZIP files containing lure documents relevant to their region (Taiwan and Czech Republic).

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Users were tricked into executing a malicious file (e.g., LNK or macro-enabled document) extracted from the initial ZIP archive.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: A PowerShell script was triggered after user execution to download the next stage of malware from an attacker-controlled server, often using encoded commands or hidden windows.

attack_patterns.name: Keylogging
attack_patterns.description: The deployed RAT or info-stealer had the capability to log keystrokes to steal information.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Collected data, such as stolen files and keylogs, was exfiltrated back to the attackers via the established command and control channel.

campaigns.name: Operation Dragon Weave
campaigns.description: A sophisticated cyberespionage campaign targeting government, technology, finance, and academic organizations in Taiwan and the Czech Republic using localized spearphishing emails with malicious ZIP attachments.
campaigns.objective: Intelligence gathering, data exfiltration, and long-term remote access.

infrastructure.name: Operation Dragon Weave C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled servers used to host second-stage malware payloads and serve as the command-and-control (C2) endpoint for the deployed RATs.

indicators.name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution via Encoded or Hidden Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Execution via Encoded or Hidden Command
id: 9c8d7e6f-5a4b-4c3d-8b2a-1f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell being launched with encoded commands or in a hidden window, a common TTP for defense evasion and payload execution as seen in Operation Dragon Weave.
references:
  - https://www.taiwannews.com.tw/news/6375749
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-03
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -w hidden'
      - ' -EncodedCommand '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of encoded or hidden window parameters in PowerShell commands, a technique used in Operation Dragon Weave to obscure malicious activity during execution.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution via Encoded or Hidden Command
